success_surface: Cell-Level Date Gap Detection and Population
effective_behaviors: Use openpyxl for cell-level modifications to preserve existing worksheet data and structure
Validate data types during row iteration to filter invalid or empty cells before processing
Determine logical range boundaries (min/max) from actual data rather than hardcoded values
Apply set difference operations to efficiently identify missing entries in sequential data
Verify analysis results through logging before committing changes to file
Write only to specified target ranges, respecting instruction constraints
Perform post-write verification to confirm all target cells contain expected values
Close workbook resources explicitly after save operations
evidence: Used load_workbook() instead of pandas.read_excel() for direct cell access and preservation
Filtered with isinstance(cell_value, datetime) to handle None/trailing empty rows correctly
Calculated min_date and max_date dynamically from extracted data, not from instruction
Generated complete date range using timedelta, then found gaps via set(all_dates) - set(existing_dates)
Printed 'Date Analysis' summary showing counts before writing any values
Wrote exactly 12 missing dates to C2:C13 as specified by answer_position
Ran verification loop after save to confirm C2-C13 values match missing_dates list
Called wb.close() after wb.save() to release file handles

memory_items.title: Sequential Data Gap Detection Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When finding missing entries in a sequential dataset, establish min/max bounds from actual data, generate complete sequence, use set difference for efficiency
memory_items.generalization: For any sequential data (dates, numbers, IDs): extract existing values -> determine range boundaries -> generate full expected sequence -> compute set differences -> write identified gaps to target location

memory_items.title: Type Validation During Data Extraction
memory_items.lesson: Always validate data types when iterating rows because trailing/empty cells may contain None or incorrect types
memory_items.generalization: When reading values from spreadsheet columns: check isinstance() for expected types, skip None/NaN, filter before processing to prevent errors downstream

memory_items.title: Iterative Exploration Before Implementation
memory_items.lesson: First examine workbook structure (headers, row count, column count, sample values) before writing solution code
memory_items.generalization: Pattern: Load workbook -> inspect dimensions and headers -> sample data types -> then implement transformation logic based on discovered schema

memory_items.title: Verification Loop After Cell Writes
memory_items.lesson: Read back newly written cells immediately after save to confirm they contain expected values
memory_items.generalization: After writing to target cells/ranges: iterate same positions, compare values against expected result, log any mismatches before considering task complete

edits.content:
## Advanced Patterns

### 7. Sequential Data Gap Detection
- **When to use**: Finding missing entries in ordered sequences (dates, numeric ranges, IDs)
- **Method**: Extract existing values -> determine min/max bounds -> generate complete expected sequence -> apply set difference operation
- **Efficiency**: Use Python sets for O(1) lookup rather than nested loops
- **Implementation**: Generate full range first, then `missing = set(expected) - set(extracted)`

### 8. Type-Aware Data Reading
- **Problem**: Spreadsheets often have mixed content (None, NaN, strings, dates) in same column
- **Solution**: Validate with `isinstance()` checks during iteration
- **Filter early**: Skip non-matching types before adding to processing lists
- **Handle edge cases**: Check for None before type checking to avoid AttributeError

### 9. Verification After Write Operations
- **Always**: Read back newly written cells immediately after save
- **Compare**: Match written values against expected result programmatically
- **Log discrepancies**: Print any mismatches with cell addresses for debugging
- **Don't assume**: Saving does not guarantee correct values were written

### 10. Target Range Constraint Adherence
- **Respect answer_position**: Write only to specified cell ranges from instructions
- **Calculate bounds**: Derive end row from start row + count of new entries
- **Avoid overwriting**: Do not write beyond the specified range even if more data exists
